auditQuestion: If we enabled live executeOne today, what would still prevent this system from operating safely without manual intervention?
finalRecommendationReason: Safety layers and dry-run validation are strong, but P109 recruiter mapping approvals are not wired into the P106.3 live classifier/runner, 121 mapping reviews remain pending, and executive or operational gates (P99/P84/operator checklist) are designed to require explicit human sign-off before executeOne. Live executeOne today would bypass the mapping-approval work completed in P113–P114 for closed-ad recovery and would still require manual intervention for the majority of the closed-ad cohort.

architecture.rationale: End-to-end pipeline is traceable and modular (ingestion → classification → mapping → runner → controlled send → Dropbox Sign → audit). Dual mapping engines (P106 closed-ad heuristics vs P108 weighted scoring) and a deliberate split between dry-run approval bridge (P109–P115) and production runner (P106.3) reduce clarity and create integration debt.

reliability.rationale: State is largely local (.data JSON/JSONL), runner overlap lock is file-based, Breezy sync failures warn but cycles continue, and there is no production-grade observability, failover, or replicated audit storage.

safety.rationale: Protection order is explicit (already_sent, duplicate_risk, invalid_email override approvals), executeOne is capped at one send per run, executeBatch is blocked in production runner policy, P100 execution locks require executive flags, and dryRun is the default everywhere.

automation.rationale: Auto-repair handles some P84 operational gates, but closed-ad project mapping still blocks ~305 candidates without P109 approval in runner, 6 REVIEW FIRST bulk groups (30 candidates) need recruiter judgment, and live send requires multi-step executive approval (P97/P99/P84/P101).

operationalReadiness.rationale: Executive UI covers mapping, bulk review, checklist, and monitor panels, but backlog is large (121 pending reviews per P110), no production alerting exists, rollback artifacts are local-only, and recruiters cannot rely on bulk SAFE path for borderline groups.

codeQuality.rationale: 625 passing tests and consistent phase-module patterns, but ~50 diagnostic scripts, overlapping mapping logic, temporary debug utilities, and large generated artifacts in-repo signal pre-production cleanup is still needed.

overallProductionReadiness.rationale: Safe to continue dry-run and controlled pilot executeOne with operator oversight; not safe for unattended production automation until P109→P106 integration, mapping backlog reduction, monitoring, and executive gates are complete.

pipeline.name: Breezy Applicant
pipeline.description: Source of truth for jobs and applicants; read-only in paperwork path.

pipeline.name: Candidate Ingestion
pipeline.description: scanBreezyPublishedPositionsBatch, merge into ingestion store, workflow backfill, optional automation engine.

pipeline.name: Paperwork Classification
pipeline.description: Ordered gate chain: already_sent → terminal → invalid_email → duplicate_risk → project mapping → P83 advancement → resume → P84 eligibility.

pipeline.name: Project Mapping (P108 analysis)
pipeline.description: Weighted 0–100 scoring, AUTO_MAP/REVIEW/NO_MATCH; feeds review queue and executive panel.

pipeline.name: Closed-Ad Recovery (P106.3 production path)
pipeline.description: Title/city/state heuristic match to published jobs; high → auto map, medium → project_mapping_review, none → project_not_mappable.

pipeline.name: Approval Bridge (P109–P115)
pipeline.description: Local recruiter decisions in .data/p109-project-mapping-review-decisions.json; bulk tools P111–P115; dry-run impact via P110.

pipeline.name: Runner Selection
pipeline.description: Incremental vs fullReconciliation; checkpointed blocked registry; stale-eligible recovery.

pipeline.name: Runner Execution
pipeline.description: Optional Breezy sync → P106 engine; file lock prevents overlap; schedule via AUTONOMOUS_PAPERWORK_RUNNER_SCHEDULE_ENABLED.

pipeline.name: executeOne
pipeline.description: Auto-repair repairable P84 gates, then executeControlledLiveSend with maxSends=1 for executeOne.

pipeline.name: Dropbox Sign
pipeline.description: prepareOnboardingSend → executeOnboardingSend; updates workflow + onboarding status.

pipeline.name: Audit Logging
pipeline.description: Append-only local audit trails for approval persistence and live sends.

pipeline.name: Ready For Onboarding
pipeline.description: Paperwork sent → signature tracking → onboarding pipeline reconciliation.

criticalIntegrationGap.description: P109 approved mappings are simulated in P110/P111–P115 but classifyPaperworkBlocker and resolveClosedAdProjectMapping do not load P109 records. Approvals in P113/P114 unlock eligibility only in dry-run overlay, not in live runner path.
evidence: No P109 imports under src/lib/p106-autonomous-paperwork-engine or closed-ad-project-mapping
P110 artifact: 6 approved mappings, 20 still need recruiter review, 305 notApproved
P109 approval-bridge documents unapprovedReviewBlocked but runner uses P106 heuristics only

protections.notes: Distinct from P108 REVIEW queue; heuristic-based.
Often surfaced as project_not_mappable rather than explicit unpublished_job category in classifier.
executeBatch disabled in P106.3 production policy.
P101 panel + API; manual executive steps.
Bridge exists for workflow UI; not consumed by live classifier.
Live mode null in all P109–P115 artifacts.

missingOrWeakProtections.description: Recruiter-approved mappings do not override project_not_mappable / project_mapping_review in live P106 path.
No automated alerts on send failure, runner errors, or audit anomalies.
P97/P100/runner audits are local JSONL without backup or tamper detection.
P108 intelligent scoring and P106 closed-ad heuristics can disagree; operators may approve in P109 while runner still blocks.
P110 newly_eligible_via_approval clears mapping gate only; candidates may still fail missing_resume or p84_gate_failed after approval.

productionBlockers.description: P113/P114 local approvals (6 candidates) do not affect classifyPaperworkBlocker or resolveClosedAdProjectMapping in the live runner.
productionBlockers.riskIfIgnored: Operators believe approved mappings will send; runner continues blocking project_not_mappable/project_mapping_review for same candidates.
productionBlockers.recommendedSolution: Integrate resolveApprovedMapping overlay into classify-paperwork-blocker (mirror P110 simulation) behind explicit feature flag; keep protection order.

productionBlockers.description: P110: 20 closed-ad candidates need recruiter review; P115: 6 REVIEW FIRST bulk groups affecting 30 candidates; 121 pending per P113 post-approval metrics.
productionBlockers.riskIfIgnored: Large closed-ad cohort cannot auto-advance; manual triage load remains extreme.
productionBlockers.recommendedSolution: Complete individual/bulk review for SAFE paths; reject or skip low-confidence groups (e.g. Elko NV per P115); wire approvals to runner after integration.

productionBlockers.description: Live executeOne requires P99 readiness, P97 audit/rollback, P84 liveSend flags, and P101 operator checklist GO.
productionBlockers.riskIfIgnored: Uncontrolled live send or bypass of executive approval chain.
productionBlockers.recommendedSolution: Run P101 checklist; complete P99 POST; enable P84 only after dry-run cohort validation.

productionBlockers.description: P106 closed-ad title match vs P108 weighted scoring produce different recommendations and confidence.
productionBlockers.riskIfIgnored: Recruiter confusion, duplicate review work, incorrect bulk approvals.
productionBlockers.recommendedSolution: Unify on P108 scoring for closed-ad resolution or document authoritative source per gate.

productionBlockers.description: P107 paperwork monitor exists but no paging/alerting on runner failure, send errors, or stale ingestion.
productionBlockers.riskIfIgnored: Silent failures; candidates stuck without operator awareness.
productionBlockers.recommendedSolution: Wire monitor metrics to alerting (email/Slack); dashboard SLOs for runner health.

productionBlockers.description: Ingestion, P109 approvals, P100 sent state, runner lock all in .data on one host.
productionBlockers.riskIfIgnored: Data loss or split-brain if multiple instances deploy.
productionBlockers.recommendedSolution: Document single-runner deployment; plan DB or object-store migration for production.

productionBlockers.description: Approved mappings may still leave candidates blocked on missing_resume, call_first_required, or p84_gate_failed.
productionBlockers.riskIfIgnored: False expectation that mapping approval equals send-ready.
productionBlockers.recommendedSolution: Executive UI should show full gate stack post-approval; extend P110 report to surface overlay blockers prominently.

productionBlockers.description: Runner cycle depends on Breezy ingestion; sync warnings do not halt cycle.
productionBlockers.riskIfIgnored: Stale job/candidate data leads to wrong mapping or send attempts.
productionBlockers.recommendedSolution: Hard-fail runner when sync fails in live mode; surface capture health in operator checklist.

productionBlockers.description: scripts/p102-execute-one-live-send.ts and many p6x–p10x validation scripts are not production entrypoints.
productionBlockers.riskIfIgnored: Operator runs wrong script in production.
productionBlockers.recommendedSolution: Document canonical runbook; move experimental scripts to scripts/experimental/ or archive.

operationalReadiness.summary: UI supports individual and bulk mapping review, but 121 pending items, 6 REVIEW FIRST groups with no splittable SAFE subgroups, and disconnect between approvals and runner blockers make fully autonomous operation unrealistic today.

projectMappingWorkflow.notes: P108 panel + P109 workflow panel; large backlog.

bulkApprovals.notes: P111–P114 SAFE path proven for 6 candidates; P115 blocks bulk for 30 borderline candidates.

individualApprovals.notes: P109 store + API functional; local only.

executiveVisibility.notes: Many executive panels; no single production readiness dashboard.

monitoring.notes: P107 monitor dry-run; no production alerting.

rollbackPlan.notes: P97 rollback JSONL exists; not validated in prod.

findings.detail: Closed-ad mapping (P106) vs intelligent mapping (P108) overlap.
Large JSON artifacts (p111–p115) committed for phase validation; consider CI artifacts only.

cleanupRecommendations: Consolidate mapping engines or document single source of truth.
Remove temporary debug modules after send flow stable.
Add P116-style audit script to CI without modifying production code paths.

goLiveChecklist.item: Environment variables configured (Breezy, Dropbox Sign, runner flags)
goLiveChecklist.notes: .env.local pattern; production secrets management not audited.

goLiveChecklist.item: Mapping approvals complete for target cohort
goLiveChecklist.notes: 6 approved locally; 121+ pending; P109 not wired to runner.

goLiveChecklist.item: Dropbox Sign validated in target environment
goLiveChecklist.notes: Tests exist; prod validation not confirmed in audit.
Panels present; production readiness view incomplete.

validation.notes: Read-only audit; no production behavior modified.

warnings: P116 — read-only engineering audit after P106–P115.
P116 — no sends, no Breezy writes, no live mode enabled.
P116 — findings based on codebase inspection and committed phase artifacts.
